channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Ultrasound hearing in hedgehogs could inform new conservation methods
item.description: Hedgehogs are among Europe’s most beloved wild mammals, yet their populations are in sharp decline, with the European hedgehog now listed as ‘near threatened’ on the IUCN Red List for Europe. A major ...

item.title: NACC identifies corruption in Robodebt scheme, highlights systemic flaws in public service
item.description: The National Anti-Corruption Commission (NACC) has released its investigation into six individuals referred by the Royal Commission into the Robodebt Scheme, including five bureaucrats and former Prim...

item.title: How Technology is Helping Tanzanian Students Learn Practical Skills
item.description: Dar es Salaam. Learning technology skills like coding, robotics, and artificial intelligence (AI) in schools is no longer just about preparing students for future tech careers. According to educators ...

item.title: NCDMB Partners Radisson and Edison for 204-Room Hotel in Yenagoa
item.description: The Nigerian Content Development and Monitoring Board (NCDMB) don officially sign international management agreement (IMA) with Radisson Hospitality from Belgium and Edison Hotel and Property Developm...

item.title: Australian Fashion Designer Secures Trademark Against Pop Star Katy Perry
item.description: An Australian fashion designer named Katie Perry has successfully defended her trademark against the internationally famous pop singer Katy Perry in a legal dispute. The conflict arose because the des...

item.title: Bangladesh beat Pakistan by eight wickets in ODI opener with Nahid Rana’s five-for
item.description: In a dominant display at Mirpur, Bangladesh’s young pace sensation Nahid Rana turned heads as he claimed his maiden five-wicket haul in one-day internationals, helping his team to a comfortable eight-...

item.title: Challenges faced by eThekwini water and electricity meter readers causing estimated billing
item.description: The eThekwini Municipality has highlighted the various difficulties its water and electricity meter readers face, which have contributed to a significant number of estimated bills. According to a Fina...

item.title: Sydney Kings secure narrow 105-104 win over Perth Wildcats in NBL semi opener
item.description: The Sydney Kings have taken a crucial first step towards the NBL championship series with a nail-biting 105-104 victory over the Perth Wildcats, giving them a 1-0 lead in the best-of-three semi-final ...

item.title: Trump Makes Critical Comment About Canada’s Prime Minister Amid Domestic Policy Posts
item.description: On March 10, 2026, former U.S. President Donald Trump made headlines for targeting Canada while posting about domestic environmental issues. On his Truth Social platform, Trump referred to Canadian Pr...

item.title: Senators Urge Swift Court Action to Implement Sugar Act and Support Farmers
item.description: A group of Kenyan Senators from sugar-growing regions has petitioned Chief Justice Martha Koome to expedite court cases that are delaying the implementation of the Sugar Act No.11 of 2024. Led by Sena...

item.title: DA proposes P10-B fund to support farmers and fisherfolk fuel subsidies
item.description: The Department of Agriculture (DA) is exploring the possibility of utilizing a P10-billion portion of the Presidential Fund to provide additional support to Filipino farmers and fisherfolk through fue...

item.title: Michael Ilesanmi and Angela Deem finalise divorce after four years of marriage
item.description: After one long year of legal fight, Michael Ilesanmi, the Nigerian reality show star from '90 Day Fiancé', and his American wife, Angela Deem, don finally end their marriage officially. The two of the...

item.title: Kozhikode Police Advise Public to Verify IMEI Before Buying Second-Hand Phones
item.description: The Kozhikode city police have advised the public to thoroughly check the IMEI details of second-hand mobile phones on the Sanchar Saathi portal before making a purchase. This step is essential to ens...

item.title: Karachi Implements Extensive Security for Youm-i-Ali Procession
item.description: KARACHI: The city of Karachi is under tight security as it observes Youm-i-Ali, marking the martyrdom day of Hazrat Ali. Main routes, including M.A. Jinnah Road, have been closed to traffic to facilit...

item.title: Tanzania improves availability of medicines and medical supplies across health facilities
item.description: The Ministry of Health in Tanzania has made strong improvements in ensuring availability of medicines, medical supplies, diagnostics, and other essential health commodities in the country. Health Mini...

item.title: Pakistan’s KSE-100 index slips after morning gains fade
item.description: Pakistan’s benchmark KSE-100 index closed in the red on Wednesday, shedding 318.65 points, roughly 0.2 percent, to finish at 155,858.47 points compared to its previous close of 156,177.12. Market open...

item.title: Court of Appeal adjourns PDP Ibadan convention dispute, encourages peaceful resolution
item.description: On Wednesday, Court of Appeal wey dey sit for Ibadan adjourn the matter wey concern the Peoples Democratic Party (PDP) and their Ibadan elective convention. The court give the parties leave to try set...

item.title: Mercy Eke Collaborates with OPPO to Promote the Newly Launched Reno15 Series in Nigeria
item.description: Global smartphone brand OPPO don partner with popular Nigerian reality TV star and businesswoman Mercy Eke to shine light on their latest OPPO Reno15 Series. This new phone line na for modern creators...

item.title: President Samia officially receives abandoned baby girl in Dodoma
item.description: Dodoma: The President of the United Republic of Tanzania, Dr Samia Suluhu Hassan, has officially welcomed a newborn baby girl who was found abandoned in Nzega town, Tabora Region. The child, named Gra...

item.title: Yellow warning for strong winds issued for northern UK regions
item.description: The Met Office has issued a yellow weather warning for strong winds affecting parts of northern England, north Wales, and Scotland. The alert will be in place from 5am until 8pm on Thursday, with gust...
